canto_title: The Encounter with Piccarda Donati and the Harmony of Divine Will

scenes.summary: Dante describes the overwhelming sensation of the light and his initial confusion.
Dante observes the faces of the spirits appearing like reflections in glass or water.
Dante notices the spirits are real and sees Beatrice smiling at him.
Beatrice instructs Dante not to be surprised and tells him to trust his eyes.
Dante addresses the spirits, asking for their names and their current state.
Piccarda introduces herself as a former nun.
Piccarda explains her position in the lowest sphere of Heaven due to unfulfilled vows.
Dante asks if the souls in this sphere desire a more exalted place in Heaven.
Piccarda explains that their desires are limited by their love for God's will.
Piccarda describes how the souls' wills are perfectly aligned with the divine will.
Dante reflects on the profound clarity of the truth he has just learned.
Piccarda tells the story of a woman who entered a convent to follow a religious life.
Piccarda describes a second woman whose religious veil was forcibly removed.
The spirits begin to sing the 'Ave Maria,' causing the vision to fade like sinking objects.
Dante attempts to look back at Beatrice but finds her light too intense to bear.
